content.problemStatement:
Dream abstracts the fabric of spacetime as a directed rooted tree (arborescence) with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). Node 111 is the root and for each iii (1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N-11≤i≤N−1), the parent of node i+1i+1i+1 is fif_ifi​. All edges of this tree are directed away from the root.

Then, Dream employs a magical superpower and adds MMM directed edges to this tree in such a way that the resulting directed graph remains acyclic (a DAG).

Let's call a node of this DAG an event and further call a simple path on this DAG an era. Dream considers a pair of events (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) to be plausible if there is an era whose first event is iii and last event is jjj. Note that i<ji \lt ji<j does not have to hold for a plausible pair.

Dream now wants you to answer QQQ queries. In each query, he gives you two positive integers lll and rrr, where l≤rl \leq rl≤r, and he wishes to know the number of plausible pairs of events (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that l≤i<j≤rl \leq i \lt j \leq rl≤i<j≤r.
constraints: 1≤fi≤N1 \le f_i \le N1≤fi​≤N for each valid iii
the graph described on the input is acyclic

Subtasks**Subtask #1 (10 points):** the graph is a line ― that is, M=0M = 0M=0 and there is a permutation p1,p2,…,pNp_1,p_2,\ldots,p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​ such that p1=1p_1=1p1​=1 and nodes p1,p2,…,pNp_1,p_2,\ldots,p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​ form a directed path in the tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains N−1N-1N−1 space-separated integers f1,f2,…,fN−1f_1, f_2, \ldots, f_{N-1}f1​,f2​,…,fN−1​.
MMM lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv describing an additional edge from node uuu to node vvv.
The following line contains a single integer QQQ.
QQQ lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers lll and rrr describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of plausible pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that l≤i<j≤rl \leq i \lt j \leq rl≤i<j≤r.
